reasoning: As of the May 2025 cutoff, only two months remain before the July 2025 deadline. South Sudan has not demonstrated any recent legislative momentum toward ratifying the AfCFTA; the transitional parliament remains heavily focused on domestic political issues, such as the 24-month extension of the transitional government and economic fallout from the conflict in neighboring Sudan. Furthermore, South Sudan lacks foundational technical prerequisites, having not submitted its provisional tariff schedules. The necessary bureaucratic steps for ratification—including parliamentary approval, presidential assent, and formal deposition of instruments at the AU—require significant time and political capital. Given the continuous delay since signing in 2018 and the absence of any ongoing legislative progress, it is highly unlikely South Sudan will ratify the agreement before July 1, 2025.
batch_queries: "South Sudan" parliament OR legislative assembly ratify AfCFTA 2024 OR 2025
"South Sudan" AfCFTA expert analysis OR opinion OR economic implications
AfCFTA ratification list tracker African Union South Sudan data
"South Sudan" AfCFTA delay OR challenges OR obstacles OR postponed
